sourceTitle: Understanding how Q&A finds answers can help you get better results

questions.question: What is Notion Q&A primarily designed to do?

options.text: Generate random creative writing based on prompts
options.explanation: Q&A is not a creative writing tool; it answers questions using workspace content.

options.text: Search a workspace, synthesize information, and provide a natural‑language answer
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A searches pages you can access, combines relevant content, and returns a clear response.

options.text: Automatically organize your pages into folders
options.explanation: Organization is a manual task; Q&A does not rearrange pages.

options.text: Edit the content of pages directly from the chat window
options.explanation: Q&A provides answers but does not edit page content.

questions.question: When you type a question, how does Q&A initially process it?

options.text: It extracts only the exact keywords and ignores context
options.explanation: Q&A looks at overall meaning, not just isolated keywords.

options.text: It formulates a search query based on the overall meaning of the question
options.explanation: Correct – the AI creates a query that reflects the intent, not just the words.

options.text: It searches only page titles for a literal match
options.explanation: Q&A also examines page content and document paths, not just titles.

options.text: It sends the question to an external internet search engine
options.explanation: Q&A only works inside your Notion workspace; it does not query external sources.

questions.question: Which of the following types of Notion content does Q&A currently NOT search?

options.text: Standard pages with text blocks
options.explanation: Q&A can search regular pages containing text.

options.text: Embedded PDFs and website pages
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A cannot read the text inside embedded files; the text must be copied into a block.

options.text: Pages you have permission to view
options.explanation: Q&A searches any page you can access.

options.text: Pages that have been recently edited
options.explanation: Recent edits actually improve discoverability; Q&A does search these pages.

questions.question: What factors does Q&A consider when ranking the handful of pages it returns?

options.text: Page title length and number of images
options.explanation: Title length and image count are not mentioned as ranking signals.

options.text: Recency of updates, number of visits, and relevance to the query
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A favors pages that are recently edited, frequently viewed, and closely match the question.

options.text: The alphabetical order of page names
options.explanation: Alphabetical order is irrelevant to relevance.

options.text: Whether the page owner has a premium Notion subscription
options.explanation: Subscription status does not affect Q&A ranking.

questions.question: Why might Q&A be unable to answer a request like “Count the number of tasks completed by Valerie this week”?

options.text: Because Q&A cannot access private pages
options.explanation: The limitation is not about privacy; it’s about database handling.

options.text: Because databases are collections of pages and Q&A does not currently understand them as a single data set
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A doesn’t yet process database queries, so aggregations like counts aren’t possible.

options.text: Because the phrase “this week” is too vague for Q&A
options.explanation: Time‑specific phrases are actually helpful; the issue is the database nature of the request.

options.text: Because Q&A only works with external knowledge bases, not internal Notion content
options.explanation: Q&A works exclusively with internal workspace content.

questions.question: Which practice improves the likelihood that Q&A will surface a page in its results?

options.text: Leaving the page title blank so Q&A can infer the content
options.explanation: Untitled pages are harder for Q&A to match.

options.text: Giving each page a meaningful, descriptive title
options.explanation: Correct – clear titles help Q&A understand relevance.

options.text: Embedding all important information as PDFs only
options.explanation: Q&A cannot read embedded PDFs; the text must be in a block.

options.text: Hiding the page from the navigation bar
options.explanation: Visibility in navigation does not affect Q&A’s ability to search the page.

questions.question: How does regularly refreshing a page’s content affect Q&A’s ranking algorithm?

options.text: It lowers the page’s priority because it appears less stable
options.explanation: Fresh content actually increases priority.

options.text: It boosts the page’s authority because recent edits and visits are weighted higher
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A favors pages that have been recently edited and frequently viewed.

options.text: It has no impact; Q&A only looks at the original creation date
options.explanation: Both creation and last edited dates are considered.

options.text: It causes Q&A to skip the page entirely to avoid duplicate results
options.explanation: Refreshing does not cause exclusion; it improves discoverability.

questions.question: What is a recommended way to handle outdated pages so they don’t interfere with Q&A results?

options.text: Delete them permanently from the workspace
options.explanation: Deletion is optional, but the guide suggests a softer approach.

options.text: Add a word like “outdated” or “deprecated” to the page title and optionally move them to an Archive page
options.explanation: Correct – labeling helps Q&A recognize that the content may no longer be current.

options.text: Convert them into PDFs and embed the PDFs on a new page
options.explanation: Embedding PDFs makes the content unreadable to Q&A.

options.text: Leave them unchanged but restrict access to a single user
options.explanation: Access restrictions limit visibility, but labeling is the recommended practice.

questions.question: Which phrasing technique is most likely to produce a precise answer from Q&A?

options.text: “What happened at the design standup?”
options.explanation: Too generic; lacks time context.

options.text: “What happened at the design standup today?”
options.explanation: Correct – adding a time‑specific phrase narrows the search to recent content.

options.text: “Tell me everything about design.”
options.explanation: Very broad; Q&A may return many pages without focus.

options.text: “Give me the onboarding process.”
options.explanation: Lacks specificity such as audience or format, which reduces precision.

questions.question: Compared to the AI Writer, what is a key limitation of Notion Q&A?

options.text: Q&A cannot access any information outside of the user’s workspace
options.explanation: Correct – Q&A is limited to workspace content, whereas AI Writer can draw on broader knowledge.

options.text: Q&A does not support natural‑language responses
options.explanation: Q&A does provide natural‑language answers.

options.text: Q&A cannot be used on mobile devices
options.explanation: Q&A is available across devices.

options.text: Q&A requires users to write complex query syntax
options.explanation: Q&A is designed for plain, conversational questions.
